rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report 'named thing must be an expression with a type' or '<error>' types, resolve the first unresolved identifier on the same line and avoid adding new architecture elements.
For implementation-level signal routing, ensure all referenced ports and variables are properly declared and visible in the current scope before using them in assignments.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference features or variables that are explicitly declared or visible in the same annex scope.
Assignments in implementation contracts must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
When resolving reference errors, trace back to the root cause of the unresolved identifier and fix it at the source.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantee expressions must have matching types on both sides of '='.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annex expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to add new component ports or features to resolve AGREE type errors; instead, correct the reference or remove the invalid clause.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings only.
